October L6, l-990
Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Politica1 Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
Re: Government Code S 871-03.5 Income from
Retail Sales Exception to Conflict of Interest
Provisions of the Political Reform Act
Dear Ms. Pritchard:
This letter reguests advice from the Fair PoIitical
Practices Commission, pursuant to Government Code S 831-14(b).1 f
am requesting informal assistance as a person with a duty to
advise other persons relating to their actions under the
Political Reform Act (2 California Code of Regulations,
S L832e (c) ) .
Facts
A city council candidate owns and controls a weekly
newspaper. A corporation places advertisements for itself,
unrelated to the election, in the newspaper at a cost in excess
of $250. The candidate wins the election and becomes a city
counci] member. Within l-2 months of the advertising purchase, a
natt.er cones before the city council v,,hich has a material
financial effect on the corporation, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally.
If the advertising purchase renders the corporation a
rrsource of incomett to the council member (S 87L03(c)), then the
council member woutd have a financial interest in a decision
which has a foreseeable, material financial effect on the
corporation (S 87L03). Because the council member may not be
involved in decisions in which he knows he has a financial
interest, h€ would ordinarily have to disqualify himself from the
decision.
1aII statutory references are to the California Government
Code, unless otherwise sPecified.


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practi.ces cornrnission
October L6, l99O
Page 2
However, if the advertising purchase could fit into the
rrincome from retaiL salesrr exception (S 87103.5), then the
corporation would not be a source of income and the council
member would not have to disgualify hinself from the decision.
ouestions Presented
1,. Is the corporation considered a rrsource of incomerr to the
council rnernber by virtue of the advertising purchase
(S 87103 (c) )?
2. 
If the answer to Question 1 is yes, can the rrincome from
retail salesrt exception be applied so the corporation might
not be a rrsource of incomerr (S 87103.5)?
Discussion
In the Cornmission's smith letter (I-88-456, I/25/89), l-.he
rrincome from retail salesrr exception was applied to a flower shop
which sold floi"rers to the public. 
A newspaper selIs advertising
space and subscriptions to the public and appears to be a
business entity engaged in retail sales. Accordingly, if the
nevrspaper's retaj-I customers constitute a significant segment of
the public generalLy, and the amount of income received by the
newspaper from the corporation is not distinguishable from the
amount of income received from its other retail customers, the
corporationrs advertising purchase should not be a source of
incorne to the council member.
If you have any questions or require any additional
information, please feel free to contact me.
Very trufy yours,
B^AE$€-r*
nradl\. Hertz P

sections.question:
1. Is the corporation considered a "source of income" to the council member by virtue of the advertising purchase (S 87103(c))?
2. If the answer to Question 1 is yes, can the "income from retail sales" exception be applied so the corporation might not be a "source of income" (S 87103.5)?

sections.question_synthetic: Whether advertising purchases by a corporation in a city council member's newspaper constitute a "source of income" triggering conflict of interest disqualification, and whether the "income from retail sales" exception under Government Code Section 87103.5 applies to newspaper advertising income.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to the FPPC seeking informal advice, not an FPPC response letter. Contains detailed Facts and Discussion sections but no CONCLUSION from FPPC. Document dates from October 16, 1990, with mixed format elements (letterhead, formal letter structure).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Is the corporation considered a "source of income" to the council member by virtue of the advertising purchase (S 87103(c))?
2. If the answer to Question 1 is yes, can the "income from retail sales" exception be applied so the corporation might not be a "source of income" (S 87103.5)?

embedding.summary: A law firm requests informal FPPC advice regarding whether a city council member who owns a newspaper must disqualify himself from voting on matters affecting a corporation that placed advertisements in the newspaper, and whether the retail sales exception applies to newspaper advertising income.
